ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a cold brew coffee class but no specific entry for vanilla sweet cream cold brew. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for vanilla sweet cream cold brew. A0CKY (coffee beverage) used as closest parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT has no specific concept for vanilla sweet cream cold brew; 34731007 (Coffee substance) used as closest parent.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard grande serving of Vanilla Sweet Cream Cold Brew is approximately 473 ml (490 g), as typically served at Starbucks.

nutrient_source.title: Starbucks Vanilla Sweet Cream Cold Brew, Grande (16 fl oz) — USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values derived from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for Starbucks Vanilla Sweet Cream Cold Brew (Grande, 16 fl oz). Values converted to per-100g basis from the 473 ml serving. Per 473 ml serving: energy ~200 kcal, protein ~2 g, fat ~11 g, saturated fat ~7 g, carbohydrate ~24 g, sugar ~23 g, dietary fibre ~0 g, sodium ~115 mg. Per 100 g calculated accordingly.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of dairy-containing coffee beverages and sweetened cold coffee drinks — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for sweetened dairy coffee beverages
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Vanilla Sweet Cream Cold Brew. GI estimated at 40 (low category) based on analogous sweetened dairy coffee beverages in the University of Sydney GI Database. The beverage contains approximately 24 g carbohydrate per 490 g serving (predominantly from vanilla syrup and sugar). Dairy fat and protein in the sweet cream component are known to attenuate glycemic response. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (40 × 24.5) / 100 ≈ 9.8.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~115 mg per serving) and caffeine
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that each grande serving contributes approximately 115 mg sodium and a significant caffeine dose from cold brew; moderate consumption is advisable and high-frequency intake should be discussed with a clinician.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar from vanilla syrup (~23 g per serving) and moderate glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should note that a grande serving contains approximately 23 g of added sugar primarily from vanilla syrup, contributing a glycemic load of approximately 9.8; requesting fewer pumps of syrup or a sugar-free vanilla option can substantially reduce the glycemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from heavy cream (~7 g per serving) and added sugar
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should note that the vanilla sweet cream float contributes approximately 7 g of saturated fat per grande serving, which represents a meaningful proportion of the recommended daily limit; limiting frequency or requesting a lighter cream option is advisable.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Vanilla Sweet Cream Cold Brew is a cold coffee beverage popularised by Starbucks, consisting of slow-steeped cold brew coffee topped with a house-made vanilla sweet cream float. Cold brew is prepared by steeping coarsely ground coffee beans in cold or room-temperature water for an extended period (typically 12–24 hours), producing a smooth, low-acidity concentrate with a naturally sweet, chocolatey flavour profile. The vanilla sweet cream is a lightly sweetened mixture of heavy cream, 2% milk, and vanilla syrup that is gently poured over the cold brew to create a visually distinct layered float. As the drink is consumed, the cream gradually blends into the coffee, creating a rich, creamy, and mildly sweet flavour. In Singapore, this beverage is available at Starbucks outlets and is priced at approximately SGD 8.50. Nutritionally, a grande serving (473 ml) provides approximately 200 kcal, 11 g fat (7 g saturated), 24 g carbohydrate (23 g sugar), 2 g protein, and 115 mg sodium. The high added-sugar content from vanilla syrup is a key consideration for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. The saturated fat from heavy cream is relevant for cardiovascular risk management. Cold brew coffee itself contains significant caffeine, which may be relevant for individuals with hypertension or cardiac arrhythmias. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 40 (low category), moderated by the fat and protein content of the dairy cream component.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are derived from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods for the Starbucks Vanilla Sweet Cream Cold Brew (Grande), which is an exact product match. GI is an analogous estimate as no direct peer-reviewed GI measurement exists for this specific beverage. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Per-100g values calculated from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods data for Starbucks Vanilla Sweet Cream Cold Brew Grande (16 fl oz / 473 ml serving): energy 200 kcal, protein 2 g, fat 11 g, saturated fat 7 g, carbohydrate 24 g, sugar 23 g, dietary fibre 0 g, sodium 115 mg. Division by 4.73 (100g basis from 473g serving) yields the per-100g figures reported. Trans fat is 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA source). Dietary fibre is 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA source — cold brew coffee and dairy cream contain no meaningful dietary fibre). Monounsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not separately reported in the USDA Branded Foods entry for this product. Polyunsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not separately reported in the USDA Branded Foods entry for this product. Potassium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. Calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. Iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. Cholesterol_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. Vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. Vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. Vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. Vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. Vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. Vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. Folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. Vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. Vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. Phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. Magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. Zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. Selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. Water_g is -1 as it is not reported in the source database entry. Glycemic index set to 40 (low category) as an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database sweetened dairy coffee beverage entries; no direct GI measurement exists for this product. GL per serving = (40 × 24.5) / 100 = 9.8. This is a Starbucks-branded commercial beverage available in Singapore at approximately SGD 8.50. The multilingual names provided (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are functional translations of the English product name as this is a Western commercial coffee beverage without native-language canonical names. The cuisine category is Coffee/Western; Singaporean multilingual naming rules applied given the field survey data source context.
